sanskrit:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता।
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः॥
transliteration: yathā dīpo nivātastho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā | yogino yatacittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ ||
literal_meaning: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker—such is the simile remembered for the yogī of controlled mind, engaged in uniting the self through yoga.
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a visceral image: when attention is no longer blown about by the winds of craving, fear or memory, consciousness becomes steady like a flame in still air. This is the baseline neuropsychology of absorbed concentration (samādhi).
psychological_state: Parasympathetic dominance; default-mode network quieting; meta-awareness stabilizes; intrusive affective charges lose momentum.
trigger_scenario: You sit down to work, phone buzzes, news headlines scream—your mind flares like a candle in a storm. You silence notifications, breathe, and within minutes the same mind is a steady flame; code flows, words arrive, time dilates.
anchor_text: Keep your mind steady like a flame in still air.
